failure_surface: Incomplete categorization: only 44 of 52 transactions were categorized, indicating missed vendor-category mappings from non-standard column locations in the source spreadsheet
root_cause: The agent assumed a single fixed-column mapping structure (Columns A-B) without detecting that vendor-category pairs may be distributed across multiple column pairs in the spreadsheet. The code used hardcoded iloc indices [0, 1] rather than dynamically identifying all vendor-category column pairs.
minimal_fix_hypothesis: Before building the mapping dictionary, scan the entire worksheet structure programmatically to identify all column pairs that contain vendor-name to category relationships, then aggregate mappings from all discovered pairs.
evidence: Spreadsheet shows multiple vendor-category column pairs visible in preview: Columns A-B ('AMAZON.COM'/'amazon'), Columns D-E ('TRANSAMERICA INS PREM'/'life insurance'), and additional unnamed columns
Agent explicitly states mapping creation uses only 'row.iloc[0] # Column A' and 'row.iloc[1] # Column B', ignoring other potential mapping columns
Output reports 'Categorized 44 out of 52 transactions' - 8 transactions remained uncategorized, suggesting their vendors existed in unmapped column regions
Only 36 vendor-category mappings were created despite multiple apparent column pairs containing vendor information

memory_items.title: Detect Multi-Pair Mapping Structures
memory_items.lesson: When extracting reference tables from spreadsheets, scan all columns for repeated patterns of key-value pairs rather than assuming a single fixed location.
memory_items.generalization: Always inspect column headers and data patterns programmatically to discover all mapping regions before constructing lookup dictionaries.

memory_items.title: Validate Coverage Before Processing
memory_items.lesson: After building any reference mapping, verify coverage by checking how many input records can be matched versus remain unmatched, and investigate the gaps.
memory_items.generalization: Report mismatch counts and analyze reasons for unmapped entries to catch incomplete reference table extraction early.

memory_items.title: Preserve All Non-Data Column Context
memory_items.lesson: When a spreadsheet contains mixed content (mapping tables alongside transaction data), preserve awareness of which columns serve which purpose.
memory_items.generalization: Dynamically classify columns by content pattern (headers vs data, short names vs categories) instead of relying on positional assumptions.

edits.content:
## 6. Mapping Table Discovery
- **Multi-Pair Detection**: When extracting reference/mapping tables from spreadsheets, do not assume a single column pair location. Scan all columns programmatically to detect repeated key-value pair patterns.
- **Pattern Matching**: Identify vendor-category structures by recognizing alternating non-null/null column patterns or header-based indicators across the entire sheet.
- **Coverage Validation**: After building any mapping dictionary, report the ratio of input records successfully matched vs. unmapped. Investigate mismatches to confirm complete reference extraction.
